title: Tensor Not Moved to GPU
description:
## The Situation

Your feature extraction script runs perfectly on your laptop, but the moment it hits the GPU server it dies:

```
RuntimeError: Expected all tensors to be on the same device,
but found at least two devices, cuda:0 and cpu!
```

The model loads. The data loads. Nothing looks obviously wrong. But something is on the wrong device.

## Your Task

1. Connect to the server (you already have a terminal)
2. Read `infer.py` — find the tensor that is not on the correct device
3. Fix it so the script runs without errors
4. Click **Check Solution** to verify your fix

## Useful Commands

```bash
python3 infer.py      # run the script
cat infer.py          # view the code
nano infer.py         # edit the code
nvidia-smi            # check GPU
```
hints: In PyTorch, every tensor has a device (cpu, cuda:0, etc.). When you call model(x), the model and all its inputs must be on the same device.
DataLoaders return CPU tensors by default. The pattern is: create your tensor, then move it with .to(device) before passing it to the model. Look for tensors that are created but never moved.
There is one tensor in infer.py that is created but never has .to(device) called on it. Find the line where it is created and chain .to(device) onto it, or pass device= as a keyword argument.
